criteria.criterionText: Then the test should exercise the system through an end-user surface
criteria.llmObservationSummary: The 'report.md' was generated and reviewed, which is an end-user surface. The current interaction with the LLM also constitutes an end-user surface.

criteria.criterionText: And an end-user surface may be:
criteria.llmObservationSummary: The 'report.md' fits the 'generated Markdown report reviewed by a human' surface type. The interaction with the LLM could be considered a form of 'CLI command used by a product owner' or a custom 'local app workflow' for QA.

criteria.criterionText: And the QA test should validate the actual human projection when the feature produces one
criteria.llmObservationSummary: The 'report.md' is a human projection produced by the feature, and it was reviewed as part of this QA process.

criteria.criterionText: And a Markdown report feature should be QAed by generating and inspecting the Markdown artifact
criteria.llmObservationSummary: The 'report.md' artifact was generated and inspected as part of this QA, directly fulfilling this criterion.

criteria.criterionText: And an HTML projection feature should be QAed by rendering the Markdown-to-HTML artifact before promotion
criteria.llmObservationSummary: No HTML projection or rendered Markdown-to-HTML artifact was provided in the evidence bundle for review, making it impossible to validate this criterion.
criteria.recommendedFixRoute: Provide the rendered HTML artifact for review as part of the evidence bundle.

criteria.criterionText: And unit tests alone should not satisfy end-user QA.
criteria.llmObservationSummary: This QA process is explicitly performing end-user validation through surface review, distinct from unit tests, which are noted to have passed locally.
